skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X posting, media posts, likes, retweets, direct messages, follows, profile lookups, and post management through the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.summary: Run Twitter/X automation tasks through the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.author: skills-shell

security_audit.summary: The static backtick findings are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences and inline command examples, not Ruby execution. The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 29 are false positives with no cryptographic implementation present. The remaining risk is real: the skill enables networked Twitter/X account actions through Bash(belt *), including posting, deleting, DMs, follows, likes, and retweets.

medium_findings.title: Account-Changing Social Media Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill documents belt commands that can publish posts, delete posts, like, retweet, send direct messages, and follow users on Twitter/X. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can create irreversible public or private account actions if prompts or inputs are wrong.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented app list and examples explicitly include post, delete, like, retweet, DM, and follow actions. These are expected capabilities, but they are externally visible account mutations.

medium_findings.title: Broad External CLI Execution Surface
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access for belt commands and instructs users to run multiple belt app commands. Command arguments appear fixed to inference.sh app IDs, but execution depends on a networked third-party CLI and authenticated user context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration permits belt commands, and examples show external CLI use. I did not find evidence of shell injection, but authenticated command execution is a meaningful risk.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Media URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh documentation, a hosted image, and an example media URL. These hardcoded URLs are expected for documentation, but they confirm the skill depends on external network resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in Markdown links and documentation entries. They do not show credential exfiltration or malicious endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Permission for Networked CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools field permits Bash(belt *), and the skill uses belt commands to call Twitter/X-related inference.sh apps. This pattern should require user confirmation before public posts, direct messages, follows, retweets, likes, or deletions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool permission and documented examples clearly show command execution that can trigger external account actions. The pattern is risky by capability, not by confirmed malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X actions from an AI assistant can be slow and error-prone. This skill provides ready-made belt CLI workflows for posting, media sharing, engagement, messaging, and profile lookup.
actual_capabilities: Posts text tweets through the x/post-tweet app.
Creates media posts through the x/post-create app.
Likes, retweets, deletes, and retrieves posts by ID.
Sends direct messages to a recipient ID.
Follows users and retrieves user profiles.
Combines image or video generation app outputs with Twitter/X posting.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Requires a configured Twitter/X integration in inference.sh.
Does not provide approval workflows or safety checks before account actions.
Cannot guarantee Twitter/X API availability, rate limits, or policy compliance.

use_cases.title: Schedule Campaign Posts
use_cases.description: Prepare and publish campaign updates with text or media from an assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Manage Product Launch Engagement
use_cases.description: Look up posts, like updates, retweet announcements, and follow relevant accounts during a launch.

use_cases.title: Post Generated Media
use_cases.description: Create an image or video with another inference.sh app and publish it to Twitter/X.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Text Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Post this update to Twitter/X after showing me the final text for approval: [draft text].

prompt_templates.title: Share a Media Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Twitter/X post using this message and media URL. Ask for approval before running the command.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Post and Engage
prompt_templates.prompt: Get details for this Twitter/X post ID, summarize it, and ask whether to like or retweet it.

prompt_templates.title: Publish Generated Creative
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short media concept, create the asset with an inference.sh app, then draft a Twitter/X post for approval.

output_examples.output: A concise tweet draft is prepared, then the assistant asks for approval before publishing it.

output_examples.input: Create a media post from an image URL.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a post with the supplied media link and confirms the exact text before running the belt command.
The assistant retrieves profile information and summarizes the relevant account details.

best_practices: Require explicit approval before posting, deleting, liking, retweeting, following, or sending direct messages.
Use test accounts or drafts before running workflows on production brand accounts.
Keep Twitter/X credentials and inference.sh authentication outside prompts and shared transcripts.
anti_patterns: Do not run engagement actions in bulk without human review.
Do not send direct messages from generated text without checking the recipient and message.
Do not use the skill to evade platform rules, rate limits, or consent requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to Twitter/X?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses inference.sh belt apps that can publish posts when the CLI is installed and authenticated.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: It requires belt login and a configured inference.sh Twitter/X integration. The skill does not include credentials.

faq.question: Can it post images or videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use x/post-create with a media URL from another workflow or external source.

faq.question: Can it send direct messages?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented x/dm-send app can send a message to a recipient ID.

faq.question: Is it safe for brand accounts?
faq.answer: Use it with approval gates. The skill can perform public and private account actions.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when the required CLI environment is available.
